Today i want to discuss a topic that worries me

What's wrong with the new characters legs?
I mean, they look normal, sometimes good, like mouchete. And this worries me. I saw a meme on another server about a lot of characters having weird legs, and last three characters were released have normal legsegs...

 Morimens is losing its identity in this regard

 Maybe then we'll stop killing children in every chapter? Add chests?
An open world? Become another anime gacha without blood and violence?

All what need to stop this -  breaking the next character's legs, piercing them like Clem's, or making them unable to walk, which is why they now constantly ride in a chair, or just cutting them off! Who needs legs?! It's the least i can ask for, and thank you for your work. thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content:
WWM works because the game push you in every corner to interact with skins.
The game *is* designed with skins in mind
- You launch the game, there's a popup for the new released skin
- Some NPCs (pretty rare so far) have cash shop skins
- You're a solo player ? Too bad, your dailies and weeklies ask you to play online, interact with other people
What happens when you go online ? You see all those pretty skins everywhere
Everything is made to unconsciously push you to check the shop and maybe buy something because you saw XxxDarkSasukexxX with a beautiful skin

(and I also didn't talk about the 40k$ ship you can invite ppl to)

thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content:
I'm not entirely sure why skins are even brought up in the first place, considering Light made it clear that they're already struggling with getting the actual content out on schedule 
Cosmetics worth spending money on take time and budget to produce, and I very much doubt that delaying new characters and chapters in favour of releasing an exclusive skin at +12 would make players happy and willing to spend
thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content:
I think that's another thing that's annoying to people. This desire to make more money without putting it more effort. Slapping extra effect on +12 cost nothing - well, it can screw up game's balance. That's why so many people say they would be fine with skins, since that at least looks like something actually ADDED to a game, something of value to the player.

Of course I do agree that characters should take priority. At the same time, we hear about money needed for voice acting (okay, fair) and collabs ??? and sorry, I don't think it's the 'we can't' it's more like 'we have other priorities'

thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall
Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content:
Read through most of the thread (of course, I came late, but not the point), and honestly, the initial takes were pure brainrot. Ngl, even if Elite loves to be toxic (and others), he was actually cooking for the most part. Most of what was said is just fearmongering and EoS panic with zero critical thinking about the actual meta. Even if we can't predict the state of the game in a year, Mori isn't the type of game to just brick old units and abandon the meta that easily.

On the main - First off, AA is not a "game killer" nor is it introducing P2W (which has existed in the game forever anyway). Even if AA touches everyone, F2Ps benefit too. There are plenty of easy to max (+12) units that are still used in comps today and aren't disappearing from the meta. Almost all standards are viable somewhere; in fact, current +12 std units already rival old arc 1 limiteds at ~E3 (not with everyone, but with most), and D-Tide demands a wide roster anyway due to RNG/relics/stages. Giving AA to everyone buffs standards too, which is a net positive for F2Ps, making the game easier. This is literally the trend for the last 2 global patches (the 10-12% stat buff all units in 2.4, and now AA - same logic as the Arc 2 Talents rolling out over time)
thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content:
Secondly, if AA was designed to break the F2P/Low-spender playstyle, they would've launched it on new units or at least on Xu. But she didn't get it. Her pre-buff OE was pretty mid, so they buffed it just to give some incentive to roll past E3 (LOL). That's why the drama around AA looked illogical from the start. We haven't seen huge buffs yet (and likely won't). The P2W aspect remains the same (maxing out was always a thing), and we're unlikely to see hard kit locks. Real locks are still at E1-E3; OE is rarely a hard lock, with the exception of maybe GDoll, who just becomes a universal top-tier support with it

There's a lot to yap about, but bottom line: the game isn't fundamentally changing after AA. It won't get harder or easier relative to power. Units get stronger -> account power rises -> enemies scale. The game has always been heavily reliant on spending to hit that 400+ in D-Tide, and it’s gonna stay that way for F2Ps (even if they are trying to lower the entry floor lately)
thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content:
Also btw. If you just want to play the game but not bust your ass for not having perfect meta units. You just do alert 3-4s and still get the majority of the important rewards.

The absolute bare minimum for a player every d zone is the pyramid. 

Getting the first batch of purple gnosis frags is the second breakpoint.

Past that it's just super old accounts since your hp and relic power is dictated by level.

Thank god hard mode of arc 2 gives  more xp than the older hard modes in arc 1.
thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content: Yep, I also wanted to mention the reward gap between 400, 375, and even 350 in D-Tide. While there is a difference, it's pretty negligible. Those extra resources are really only needed for people who are min-maxing Tide and need to push lvl 70-80 for multiple teams/comps , not just one
thread_title: Regarding the possible +12 Paywall

content: And I'm F2P too, just started dipping my toes into A5 and aiming for 400 (It happened once, even though it was in the easiest tide in the game, lol). Ngl, it's tough, mostly cause I lack the experience. But the kicker is - I probably have the roster for 400, just maybe not consistently (could prob hit 390-395 constant if I tryhard, but I hate sweating). So AA doesn't bother me at all. I'll keep stomping A4 like usual, and if AA buffs my units, maybe 400 will actually become easy.
